mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate reduces equity valuations by raising the discount rate applied to future cash flows. With rates already at 3.62% and trending upward, further tightening depresses S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS and SPX 20-day returns is positive in more than 60% of subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5%, the labor market is considered tight, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns in triggered instances are negative in more than 55% of cases, or the average return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial expansion (INDPRO above 115), continued strength in industrial production signals robust economic activity and rising capacity utilization, leading to higher corporate profits and positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: When INDPRO > 115, the sign of forward 20-day returns is negative more often than positive, or the IC over a 3-year rolling window is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising federal funds rate and persistently high housing starts (above 2,000 thousand units) indicates speculative excess in housing and impending demand slowdown, which negatively impacts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the economy adjusts.
failure_condition: The joint signal (FEDFUNDS rising and HOUST > 2000) produces positive median 60-day forward returns, or the rank IC of the interaction term is less than -0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 200, inflation is considered elevated, prompting expectations of tighter monetary policy that drags on equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL > 200, forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) consistently signal healthy consumer spending, which boosts corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month regardless of the economic cycle.
failure_condition: The correlation between RSAFS level and forward 20-day returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window, or the Rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP is high (GDPC1 above 11000) and the unemployment rate is low (UNRATE below 5.5%), the economy is near full capacity, and additional growth leads to wage pressures and eventual monetary tightening, hurting equities over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint condition (GDPC1 > 11000 and UNRATE < 5.5%) leads to positive median 60-day returns, or the interaction term shows a positive correlation with forward returns over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) exceed 2,000 thousand units, it marks a cyclical peak in residential investment that typically precedes a slowdown in construction and consumer durables, causing short-term negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: When HOUST > 2000, forward 5-day returns are positive in more than half of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).
